prevalence.value: Approximately 10-15% in adult women; 5-10% in general population
prevalence.population: Over 1.4 billion (focus on adult females ~700 million)
prevalence.trend: Increasing due to rising diabetes and antibiotic resistance

incidence.value: Around 11-15 million new cases annually in women alone
incidence.source: ICMR reports and WHO estimates for South-East Asia

burden.dalyPerCapita: 0.5-1.0 DALYs per 1,000 population (primarily from uncomplicated cases)
burden.mortalityRate: Low (<0.1% overall; higher in complicated cases like pyelonephritis ~1-2%)
burden.source: Global Burden of Disease Study 2021, adapted for India

vulnerablePopulations: Rural populations with limited access to clean water
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical symptoms (dysuria, frequency, urgency) + urinalysis showing pyuria (>10 WBC/hpf) and bacteriuria; urine culture for confirmation (≥10^5 CFU/mL of single pathogen); imaging (USG) for complicated cases

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: Low (<20% routine screening in primary care; higher in antenatal clinics ~50%)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate (60-70% resolution with first-line antibiotics; challenged by resistance)

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Follows ICMR guidelines: Symptom-based initial diagnosis in resource-limited settings; culture-guided in urban hospitals
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: ICMR-recommended: Nitrofurantoin or Fosfomycin for uncomplicated UTI; Ciprofloxacin or Cefixime for complicated (with local resistance patterns); emphasis on avoiding over-the-counter antibiotics
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High antimicrobial resistance (e.g., >50% E. coli resistant to fluoroquinolones); limited rural access to diagnostics; water scarcity and poor sanitation in slums; over-reliance on empirical therapy
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Robust antibiotic stewardship programs in major cities; integration with NCD programs for diabetics; growing telemedicine for remote consultations; affordable generic drugs
